title: NEET paper leak row: Delhi court sends two accused to 10-day CBI custody
content: The CBI has secured 10 days custody of accused Manisha Waghmare and PV Kulkarni in the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak case. Both are alleged to be part of a larger conspiracy to obtain and distribute leaked exam papers. Investigators claim a senior teacher leaked Biology questions, while a retired professor was involved in the question paper setting panel.

title: 'No feud with KC Venugopal': Kerala CM-designate VD Satheesan breaks silence
content: Kerala CM-designate VD Satheesan and senior leader KC Venugopal dismissed reports of internal feuds, asserting that all organizational and cabinet decisions are made jointly through consultations. The oath-taking ceremony for the new Congress-led UDF government is scheduled for May 18, with cabinet formation discussions underway.

title: Moody's Analytics chief economist Mark Zandi warns of high risk of recession in America
content: Moody's chief economist Mark Zandi warns of a 40% recession risk in the US within a year, citing 'counterproductive policy choices' like tariffs and restrictive immigration. He suggests abandoning these policies, ending the Iran war, and letting the Fed maintain steady interest rates could avert a downturn, despite slowing job growth.

title: Centre approves key NTA appointments as uproar over NEET paper leak intensifies
content: Amidst the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak scandal, the government has appointed four new officials to key positions within the National Testing Agency (NTA). The CBI continues its investigation, recently arresting a senior teacher from Pune identified as a key source of leaked Biology questions. Protests and legal challenges are escalating, with demands for accountability and the NTA's dissolution.

title: 'Sack Dharmendra Pradhan now': Rahul says NEET paper leak 'betrayed' 22 lakh aspirants
content: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi has intensified his attack on the Centre regarding the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. He is demanding the immediate removal of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. The National Testing Agency cancelled the examination due to evidence of question paper circulation. The Central Bureau of Investigation is investigating the matter. The exam will be re-conducted on June 21.

title: Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt has message for engineers who are still coding the old way
content: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt declared the era of manual code writing is over, citing AI's explosive power. He believes programmers still using traditional methods are behind, as AI enables individuals to build powerful applications previously requiring large teams. This shift is causing a sell-off in software stocks, with companies needing to adapt quickly to survive.

title: Tamil Nadu cabinet: 10 ministers get portfolios, CM Vijay keeps home, police — check full list
content: The Tamil Nadu Lok Bhavan has announced portfolio allocations for the new cabinet. Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay will lead the government. He has retained key departments. Other ministers have been assigned portfolios including finance, health, and education. This marks a significant administrative development for the new government.

title: After Microsoft gave engineers access to Anthropic's Claude Code, company is canceling licenses
content: Microsoft is phasing out internal licenses for Anthropic's Claude Code AI tool, shifting developers to its own GitHub Copilot CLI platform. This strategic move, effective by the end of June, aims to streamline AI coding tools and align with Microsoft's engineering needs and financial year-end.

title: Pune hospital bomb scare: Shoe seller held from train for planting IED to extort money
content: A 35-year-old shoe seller from Solapur, living in Pune, was arrested at Nagpur railway station for allegedly planting a low-intensity bomb at a Pune hospital. Shivaji Rathod, unable to afford treatment for a sexually transmitted disease, attempted to extort money by creating panic. He was apprehended by the ATS on a train after a manhunt.

title: ‘Not comfortable’: Virat Kohli slams invasive camera culture
content: Cricketer Virat Kohli finds constant camera monitoring during Indian Premier League practice sessions intrusive. He believes players need peace to prepare without every move being recorded and analyzed. Kohli feels this constant exposure impacts his ability to experiment freely. He calls for clearer boundaries regarding player consent and behind-the-scenes access. Kohli's concerns arise despite his strong performance this IPL season.

title: CBSE 3-language rule for Class 9 from July: What it means for students
content: Starting July 1, 2026, CBSE mandates three languages for Class IX, with at least two being Indian. No board exam will be held for the third language in Class X; assessments will be school-based. This aligns with NEP 2020 and NCF-SE 2023, ensuring smooth implementation with interim solutions for textbooks and teachers.

title: 'Not too many smart heads there': Rayudu questions CSK leadership
content: Former India batter Ambati Rayudu lamented Chennai Super Kings' lack of experienced heads on the field during their loss to Lucknow Super Giants. He highlighted young pacer Anshul Kamboj's struggles against Mitchell Marsh and Nicholas Pooran, suggesting senior players could have offered crucial support. Rayudu defended Kamboj, attributing his tough outing to exceptional batting.

title: 'Children are wealth not burden': Andhra govt to give Rs 30,000 for 3rd child, Rs 40,000 for 4th
content: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu is urging a focus on increasing the state's population, viewing children as national assets.  He announced financial incentives, offering Rs 30,000 for a third child and Rs 40,000 for a fourth, to counter declining birth rates and an aging demographic. This policy aims to address a significant demographic shift.

title: What's 'jihadi drug'? Rs 182 crore Captagon seized under Operation Ragepill
content: India has achieved a significant breakthrough in its fight against drug trafficking with the first-ever seizure of Captagon, dubbed the 'Jihadi drug', worth Rs 182 crore. Under 'Operation Ragepill', authorities intercepted 227.7 kg of the substance, destined for the Middle East, and arrested a foreign national. This operation underscores the government's zero-tolerance policy against narcotics.

title: 'In Chennai, they know': Shastri sees silver lining for CSK after LSG loss
content: Ravi Shastri urges Chennai Super Kings to view their loss to Lucknow Super Giants as a learning opportunity. He highlighted the challenging pitch and praised CSK's 187 score despite batting struggles. Shastri believes key players will bounce back and bowlers will gain valuable experience for future matches, especially at home.

title: Who is Priyadarshini Mallick? TMC leader's daughter removed from post of WBCHSE secretary
content: Priyadarshini Mallick, daughter of TMC leader Jyotipriya Mallick, has been removed as secretary of the West Bengal Council of Higher Secondary Education. This follows her failure to resign as part of a departmental restructuring. Her name had also surfaced during the ED's probe into the PDS scam involving her father

title: 'Misquoted': CJI Surya Kant clarifies 'unemployed youth cockroach' remark
content: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant clarified his remarks comparing some unemployed youth to 'cockroaches,' stating his comments were misquoted and targeted individuals using fake degrees in professions. He expressed pride in India's youth, calling them pillars of a developed nation, and emphasized his respect for them. The clarification followed controversy over his observations during a court hearing.

title: Virat Kohli reveals why RCB winning IPL title earlier 'wouldn't have felt even 5% the same'
content: Virat Kohli revealed the immense emotional weight lifted after Royal Challengers Bengaluru finally clinched their maiden IPL title after 18 years. He stated that a victory earlier in his career wouldn't have felt as special, highlighting the profound joy derived from overcoming decades of pressure and heartbreak alongside franchise icons and the city of Bengaluru.

title: 'If it continues to harbour terrorists ...': Army chief's stern warning to Pakistan
content: Army chief Upendra Dwivedi issued a stark warning to Pakistan, stating that continued harboring of terrorists and operations against India would force them to "decide whether they want to be part of geography or history." This statement comes after the first anniversary of Operation Sindoor, where Indian forces conducted precision strikes on terror infrastructure.

title: PM Modi warns Middle East war to 'wipe away' achievements of past decades
content: Prime Minister Modi warned that ongoing global conflicts and instability risk undoing decades of development and plunging populations into poverty. Addressing the Indian diaspora in The Hague, he emphasized the critical need for resilient and trusted global supply chains, highlighting India's cooperation with the Netherlands in this area. He also celebrated the diaspora's cultural preservation and India's recent economic transformation.

title: Look-out circular against Bandi Sanjay’s son: Cops form special teams in POCSO case
content: Cyberabad police have formed five special teams and issued a look-out circular for Bandi Sai Bhagirath, son of Union minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar, in a POCSO case. Investigators are using CDR and IP addresses to trace him. The case involves alleged sexual harassment and outraging modesty of a minor.

title: Kylian Mbappe drops fourth-choice bombshell amid Real Madrid crisis
content: Kylian Mbappe’s difficult start at Real Madrid has taken another dramatic turn after the French superstar publicly claimed he had been reduced to a “fourth-choice” role under interim manager Alvaro Arbeloa. The shocking comments came after Madrid’s 2-0 win over Real Oviedo, where Mbappe was again left out of the starting lineup.

title: All about Jason Statham & Rosie Huntington-Whiteley’s ‘forever home’ near an unexpected location
content: Hollywood power couple Jason Statham and Rosie Huntington-Whiteley are building a $33 million 'forever home' on England's South Coast. The striking, U-shaped brutalist design features five bedrooms, a private beach, and extensive grounds including a lake and treehouse. This move signifies Rosie's return to her rural roots, seeking a slower, grounded lifestyle away from a 'destination-driven' existence.

title: Samajwadi leader Azam Khan jailed over 2019 'tankhaiyya' remarks
content: Samajwadi Party leader Azam Khan has been sentenced to two years of simple imprisonment and fined Rs 5,000 in Rampur for making derogatory remarks against district administration officials during the 2019 Lok Sabha election campaign. The conviction was based on eyewitness testimony and video footage presented in court.

title: 'People are forced to travel in bullock carts': Congress stages protest over fuel, milk price hike
content: Congress workers protested rising fuel and milk prices, with leaders arriving by bullock cart to highlight inflation's impact. Delhi Congress president Devendra Yadav criticized the government's handling of essential commodity costs, stating inflation has doubled since 2014. This demonstration follows a recent Rs 3 per litre hike in petrol and diesel prices across major cities.

title: After Trump, Putin to visit China's Xi Jinping on May 19 for two days
content: Russian President Putin is set for a significant two-day visit to China, commencing May 19, to deepen strategic ties with President Xi Jinping. This follows closely on the heels of Donald Trump's high-profile trip to Beijing, which focused on Taiwan, trade, and Iran. The leaders will discuss strengthening their partnership and address key global issues, aiming to sign a joint declaration.

title: NEET-UG paper leak row: Doctors' body calls for dissolution of NTA, files plea in Supreme Court
content: Doctors' body UDF has petitioned the Supreme Court to dissolve the National Testing Agency, citing a "catastrophic failure" in the NEET-UG 2026 exam's conduct due to alleged paper leaks. The plea also demands significant reforms for transparency and fairness in national entrance tests, aiming to prevent future irregularities.

title: J&K: Police officer shoots wife dead after a quarrel; attempts suicide
content: A probationary sub-inspector in Jammu and Kashmir's Kupwara district allegedly shot his wife dead following a late-night quarrel. The officer, identified as Deep Singh, then reportedly shot himself and is currently in critical condition undergoing treatment. The incident occurred at an SOG camp in the Kandi area.

title: Starbucks pours a bitter brew: Plans laying off 300 workers in $400 m restructuring
content: Starbucks is laying off approximately 300 US-based roles as it consolidates its office network and shutters several locations. This move is part of a broader strategy to achieve "durable, profitable growth" by reducing complexity and costs. The company expects additional international job cuts as it reviews its global support structure.

title: NCP goes all-Pawar: Sunetra takes charge as sons get key party roles; seniors sidelined
content: NCP president Sunetra Pawar's letter to the ECI, omitting key organizational posts for Praful Patel and Sunil Tatkare, has sparked speculation. Sources indicate Sunetra Pawar and her sons are seeking a meeting with Tatkare to clarify the issue. Pawar previously attributed similar omissions to technical mistakes.

title: India Vs Pakistan: How the two nations stack up when it comes to nuclear arsenals
content: India and Pakistan possess nearly identical nuclear arsenals, yet their doctrines diverge significantly. India adheres to 'No First Use' and aims for a minimum deterrent, while Pakistan's 'Full Spectrum Deterrence' counters India's conventional strength. India is expanding its nuclear triad, focusing on survivability and reach, while Pakistan emphasises tactical nuclear weapons and flexible options, raising escalation risks.

title: Ravi Mohan halts films: Actor vows break till divorce ends; fans express sympathy
content: Actor Ravi Mohan has broken his silence on his divorce from Aarti, addressing rumors linking singer Keneeshaa Francis to their separation. He emotionally stated his silence was not weakness and vowed to not act until his divorce is finalized. Mohan also expressed pain over not being allowed to see his children.

title: INDIA bloc must align to stop the juggernaut of the BJP, says Dipankar Bhattacharya
content: Bihar Opposition leaders come together at CPI(ML)’s 12th Bihar Conference, held against the backdrop of the BJP riding to power in West Bengal

title: Choose careers based on interest and strengths, Regional Commissioner tells students
content: At The Hindu EducationPlus Career Counselling Fair-2026 in Mysuru, Regional Commissioner Nitesh Patil stresses on the importance of the right environment in shaping one’s future and urges students to take informed decisions

title: Kamal meets Vijay, submits 6 demands Tamil film industry’s development
content: The meeting comes a day after the MP called for a conversation with stakeholders regarding the adoption of ‘practical and sustainable film-making practices’

title: Fisheries dept. to deploy rescue guards at fishing harbours
content: The move is aimed at ensuring swift emergency response and improving preparedness for accidents and distress situations at sea during the rough monsoon season

title: ABC centre in Kochi struggles with ageing electrical infrastructure
content: This has affected the centre’s operations, restricting daily sterilisation surgeries for stray dogs to an average of six. The centre currently has one veterinary surgeon, two operation theatre assistants, and six animal handlers

title: Bengal Government orders re-verification of caste certificates issued since 2011
content: About 1.69 crore SC, ST and OBC certificates have been issued in the State since 2011; a letter issued by the Backward Classes Welfare Department to all District Magistrates on May 14 says about 47.8 lakh certificates, which include 32.51 lakh SC certificates, 6.65 lakh ST certificates and 8.64 lakh OBC certificates, were issued under the Duare Sarkar camps during the previous Trinamool Congress regime.

title: Comprehensive drainage package approved for NH66 across Kerala
content: As part of a comprehensive flood mitigation effort, the NHAI will fully fund the construction costs required to connect highway drainage outlets to the nearest natural waterbodies

title: Minister urges people to shun plastic
content: Janardhan Reddy says awareness programmes must be conducted to educate the public about the ill-effects of plastic and the health risks associated with it

title: Action against communal hate, anti-lynching safeguards key demands at Muslim convention in Karnataka
content: A 76-page report released at the convention reviewed the status of several promises and pending issues concerning Karnataka’s Muslim community; organisers called it part of ‘constructive dialogue’

title: Lorry runs over women labourers in Kakinada; four killed, two critical
content: The incident occurred when a group of six women were on their way to attend work under the MGNREGA, Pawan Kalyan announces ₹4 lakh compensation each to the families of the deceased

title: NEET paper leak row: Rahul Gandhi demands Dharmendra Pradhan’s removal
content: The (Parliamentary) Committee had made recommendations but he (Dharmendra Pradhan) put it in the dustbin on the pretext that opposition people were on the committee and that it was of no use, says Congress leader Rahul Gandhi

title: Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari alleges Diamond Harbour people denied voting rights for decade
content: In his first political meeting since he became Chief Minister, Mr. Adhikari said, "The Falta repolls will re-establish voter rights where people have not been able to vote for 10 years ever since Abhishek Banerjee arrived on its political scene.”

title: AP ICET-2026 records 91.41% pass rate; women outshine men
content: Over 21,000 qualify for MBA and MCA admissions; Andhra University region records higher success rate than SVU, while non-local candidates top with 95.03% pass percentage

title: 9.57 LT maize procured in State so far; govt. plans to purchase jowar too
content: Agri. Minister Tummala Nageswara Rao urges Centre to enhance procurement of bengalgram, groundnut to at least 50% of total produce from present 25%

title: From collapse to comeback: Tungabhadra dam in Karnataka gets new lease of life
content: Nearly two years after Gate No. 19 was washed away, the historic reservoir stands renewed with all 33 crest gates replaced; it is set to be inaugurated by month-end

title: Now book an appointment for bulky waste to be picked up from your doorstep
content: The Bengaluru Solid Waste Management Limited (BSWML), responsible for Solid Waste Management in the city, has launched a new mobile app ‘DClutter’ through which you can book for such a visit by waste collectors

title: ​Energy conservation top on agenda, says Minister Gottipati Ravi Kumar
content: The government has set for itself a target of two million PM Surya Ghar connections, and steps have been taken to install solar power in government offices and schools as well, he says

title: PM Modi in Netherlands LIVE: 'India’s aspirations no longer confined to its borders,' Narendra Modi tells diaspora
content: Addressing the Indian community in The Hague, Prime Minister Narendra Modi also said India is dreaming big and its youth aspires to reach the sky

title: Growing ‘attacks’ on the judiciary by ‘parasites’, CJI Surya Kant says
content: “There are parasites in society who attack the system... they don’t get any employment and don’t have any place in the profession. Some of them become media, some of them become social media, some of them become RTI activists, and they start attacking everyone,” the CJI observed

title: CJI Surya Kant clarifies ‘parasites’ remarks, says youth are pillars of country
content: "It is totally baseless to suggest that I criticised the youth of our nation. Not only am I proud of our present and future human resource, but every youth of India inspires me,”  the CJI said in a statement.

title: Slash ‘excessive’ VAT on fuel, withdraw additional levy and cess, demands APCC chief Sharmila
content: Compared to other southern States, consumers in Andhra Pradesh are shelling more money out for petrol and diesel, she says

title: Vijay Keeps Key Portfolios For Himself, KA Sengottaiyan Gets Finance
content: Vijay's decision to personally oversee these departments indicates his intention to maintain direct political ownership over welfare delivery and urban governance

title: Gold Bars Worth Rs 5.5 Crore Hidden In Pants, US Man Caught At Delhi Airport
content: Gold was fitted into the inner pockets of his trousers in such a manner that it would be difficult to detect during a routine check.

title: Bridge Collapses Amid Heavy Rain, Flooding in Assam's Barak Valley
content: Heavy and continuous rainfall wreaked havoc across Assam's Barak Valley on Friday, exposing serious concerns over infrastructure, urban drainage, and public safety.

title: 1996 Military Jet Killings: Why Team Trump Wants Cuba's Raul Castro Charged
content: On February 24, 1996, Cuban jets shot down two small planes being flown by Brothers to the Rescue, a group of Cuban exile pilots.

title: Himanta Biswa Sarma Launches India's First AI-Powered Bank Branch In Guwahati
content: The event programme was attended by Assam BJP MLA and former cabinet minister Ashok Singhal, along with Slice MD & CEO Rajan Bajaj and NPCI MD & CEO Dilip Asbe.

title: 22-Year-Old Woman Shot Dead By Boyfriend In Jharkhand: Cops
content: A 22-year-old woman was shot dead allegedly by her boyfriend in Jharkhand's Simdega district on Saturday after an altercation over some issue, police said.

title: Arunachal Government Issues Advisory Amid Glacial Lake Outburst Risk
content: Authorities have issued a public advisory urging residents and visitors in vulnerable areas of the Mago Chu and Tawang Chu basins to remain alert.

title: "Will Rebuild Party, Paint Offices If Needed": Mamata Banerjee After Poll Rout
content: At a meeting with the Trinamool Congress' contesting candidates, she said the organisation would rise again despite suffering a crushing defeat in the recently concluded assembly elections.

title: Video: Gun Pointed At Them, Jewellery Shop Staff Fight Robbers In Nashik
content: During the scuffle, the robbers repeatedly struck one of the employees on the head with the butt of a pistol.

title: Lucknow Woman Dies By Suicide, Blames Businessman, Wife, 1 Arrested
content: Ratna Singh, 31, died by suicide on May 12. In the video, she accused Sharad Singh and his wife, Pallavi Joshi, of mental torture.

title: Israeli Strikes Kill Hamas Armed Wing Chief, 'Architect' Of October 7 Attack
content: Ezzedine Al-Haddad had been killed in an Israeli strike on a residential apartment and a civilian vehicle in Gaza City on Friday.

title: 'Leaked Papers For You, Darling': Lucknow Professor Told Student, Arrested
content: The recording of calls to a woman student at the Lucknow University brought to the fore her continued harassment by an assistant professor in the name of leaked examination papers.

title: "They Will Take Out Gun": Russian Foreign Minister Warns Journalist At Delhi Briefing
content: The interaction was interrupted multiple times by a journalist who was allegedly speaking on the phone while Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov was answering questions.

title: Delhi Sets 2027 Deadline To Clear Ghazipur, Bhalswa, Okhla Garbage Mountains
content: Delhi has set a fresh deadline to flatten its giant garbage mountains, a promise residents around Ghazipur, Bhalswa and Okhla have heard before.

title: "We Spy Like Hell On Them": Trump Responds To Question On China Cyberattacks
content: When questioned by reporters on whether he spoke to Chinese President Xi Jinping about the cyberattacks that China has done in the US, President Trump said, "I did. He talked about attacks that we did in China. You know, what they do, we do too."

title: There Was Ruler's Law In Bengal, Now It Will Be Rule Of Law: Suvendu Adhikari
content: The Bengal chief minister announced that the Police Welfare Board has been dissolved because it had effectively turned into the "branch organisation" of a political party.

title: Handcuffed Inmate Attacks Cop, Tries To Flee In Punjab, Shot Dead: Police
content: A jail inmate was shot dead after he allegedly attacked an asthmatic police constable with a sharp-edged weapon and attempted to escape custody outside a hospital here on Saturday, police said.

title: US says El Nino just weeks away, earlier than expected
content: forecasts from international agencies suggest El Nino could be weeks away . it’s now likely that the weather condition will prevail during most of the four-month monsoon period as opposed to previous expectations that it’ll develop in the second half of the season .

content: a video of the incident has circulated widely on social media . lavrov initially asked the journalist to leave the conference room . but when the call continued, he issued a sharper warning . he said the phone should be put away or security personnel would take the gun out .
